bunnypetguide-text: Guide to taming Hares: How to obtain your first pet bunny. Similarly to taming other creatures, choose the right treats for a bunny, find an untamed hare , and feed it regularly until it reaches 100% tameness. Once fully tamed, you can give your new companion a name. Taming progress slowly decreases each day, so feeding a hare a single dry grass once per day will not be enough. Baby hares are generally easier to tame than adults. Treats: Wild hares and tamed bunnies enjoy a variety of treats, including vegetables, fruits, grains, and snacks that are generally considered non toxic for rabbits. Dry grass is inexpensive and can be fed frequently, but it provides only a small increase in obedience. Vegetables offer a balanced amount of obedience, while fruits provide a large boost but require a longer cooldown before the animal is willing to eat again. Baked treats can only be fed to tamed bunnies and provide strong increase in obedience. A bunny's obedience gradually decreases over time if it is not fed. What to do with your pet? As your bunny's obedience increases, you can issue commands to it. Bunnies can Sit, Lie Down, Stay or Follow you. When left alone, bunnies will wander around, search for food, and sleep. If you provide a small cushion, your bunny will look for it before going to sleep. Breeding Bunnies can breed with both wild hares and other bunnies. Breeding a bunny with a wild hare always produces an untamed wild baby hare whose generation is inherited from one of its parents. Breeding two bunnies together also produces an untamed wild baby hare, but its generation will always be at least Generation 2.
